title: Add chaos E2E tests to CI pipeline
summary: Review #2 feedback from berendt correctly addressed. W-001: .github/actions/** added to e2e_chaos path filter. W-002: ci-workflow.md updated to accurately describe skipped-dependency behavior. W-003: test_e2e_chaos_output assertion tightened to match full output declaration. W-004/W-005: test assertions scoped to $E2E_CHAOS_JOB_SECTION instead of full CI_YAML. I-003: duplicate timeout line removed. All 46 tests pass. YAML valid. No new issues introduced.

tests_checklist.item: All 46 verification tests pass (0 failed, 0 skipped)
ci.yaml is valid YAML (python yaml.safe_load succeeds)
Test assertions scoped to job section prevent false positives from other jobs
test_e2e_chaos_output uses specific pattern matching full output declaration

code_quality_checklist.item: W-001 fix: .github/actions/** added to e2e_chaos path filter
W-002 fix: ci-workflow.md condition description matches actual if: behavior
W-003 fix: test pattern specificity improved
W-004 fix: if-condition assertions scoped to job section
W-005 fix: chainsaw command assertions scoped to job section
I-003 fix: duplicate timeout line removed from ci-workflow.md

security_checklist.item: No secrets or credentials in changed files

architecture_checklist.item: Path filter change is additive and non-breaking
No structural changes to job definition

dry_yagni_checklist.item: Changes are minimal and targeted to reviewer feedback
No scope creep beyond W-001 through W-005 and I-003 fixes

fail_fast_checklist.item: Test assertions fail explicitly with descriptive messages

defensive_checklist.item: assert_contains with job section prevents false-positive test passes

suggested_improvements: docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md line 92-93 now omits .github/actions/** from the path filter description — it was updated in ci-workflow.md (line 461) but not in the chaos-e2e-tests.md companion doc. Consider adding it for consistency.

docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md line 107-108 still says 'It only runs if all dependencies succeeded' which contradicts the corrected ci-workflow.md (line 421) that now says 'no dependency failed or was cancelled. A skipped e2e-operator does not block the job.' Consider aligning the two docs.
